blockers_for_user: NVIDIA access for target-loss tokenwise connector and native systems rows.
Decision on COLM framing: workshop evidence package now, or hold for positive connector.
Any preferred source/target model pair for the first expensive cross-family connector run.
colm_workshop: Scope as source-private packet protocol plus public benchmark positives.
Use systems boundary table as accounting, not native speed.
Include gate-tree figure showing promoted, cut, alive, and blocked branches.
Cut HellaSwag receiver-improvement and TinyLlama hidden/query connector claims.
Frame negative ladder as evidence of rigor, not as solved cross-model latent reasoning.
iclr_full_paper: Positive tokenwise query/soft-prefix or cache-fuser connector on a frozen larger slice.
OpenBookQA 3B train-only receiver gate over packet-only with source-destroying controls.
At least one strict true cross-family pair, not just same-family Qwen scaling.
Seed repeats and paired uncertainty versus target-only, same-byte text, Qwen-substituted, wrong-row, and prompt/prefix controls.
Direct comparison against C2C/KVComm/KVCOMM and KV quantization byte floors.
Native NVIDIA rows for TTFT, TPOT, goodput, GPU memory, HBM/PCIe/NVLink traffic, accuracy, bytes, and source exposure.
Interpretability telemetry: rate-distortion curve, effective rank or gate patterns, and source/help/harm decomposition.

claim_boundary.lateral_branches: DiT/consistency-style iterative refinement and SAE universal feature spaces are inspirations, not current positive evidence.
claim_boundary.not_unique: Generic soft prompts, static prefix tuning, and KV/cache fusion are prior art and must be baselines.
claim_boundary.unique: Per-example source-conditioned rate-limited connector under source-destroying controls and explicit source-exposure accounting.

current_story: Fixed-byte source-private packets plus public benchmark gates and systems byte/exposure accounting.
exact_gap: A positive tokenwise learned/common-language connector and matched native systems rows are still missing.
needs_more_work: Tokenwise query/soft-prefix connector trained against target loss.
next_exact_gate: Mac-local ARC n32 target-loss soft-prefix preflight using Qwen source features and a frozen target, followed by the OpenBookQA 3B train-only receiver gate if controls pass.

nodes.claim: Tiny task packets can be evaluated with source-destroying controls.
nodes.decision: Keep as contribution, but do not call it solved latent reasoning.
nodes.evidence: ARC/OpenBookQA public-basis packet rows plus destructive controls remain the positive core.
nodes.next_action: Tie every method row to target-only, same-byte text, zero-source, wrong-row, and paired CI.

nodes.claim: ARC/OpenBookQA common-coordinate packets are the strongest benchmark evidence.
nodes.contribution: C2 public-basis benchmark gate and negative ladder
nodes.decision: Use as benchmark/evaluation contribution, not as final cross-family proof.
nodes.evidence: The live branch now separates positives from HellaSwag and TinyLlama hidden/query failures.
nodes.next_action: Add gate-tree figure and larger frozen slices when a connector clears validation.

nodes.claim: LatentWire packets are much smaller than source-state/KV transfer floors.
nodes.decision: Promote as accounting; native GPU systems win remains blocked.
nodes.evidence: 4 packet rows, 4-15B framed bytes, 51.200x floor over largest packet.

nodes.claim: Mean hidden/query caches are enough for a low-data connector.
nodes.evidence: MLP matched/Qwen/cached=0.232/0.317/0.269, CI low vs Qwen=-0.154; PCA/ridge, transport, and sparse-query variants were also negative.
nodes.next_action: Do not spend more Mac cycles on mean-cache PCA/RFF/MLP variants.

nodes.claim: A stronger non-Qwen source-choice sender fixes cross-family transfer.
nodes.decision: Cut current Llama/Phi source-choice senders; only revive with selected prompt/scoring.
nodes.evidence: Llama source/Qwen oracle=0.613, packet oracle=0.532, same-byte shadow=0.126, source-to-packet loss=0.186.
nodes.next_action: If revived, select prompt/scoring on validation before frozen test.

nodes.claim: A per-example source-conditioned tokenwise query/soft-prefix interface can beat packet-only.
nodes.decision: This is the next method gate; mean-cache proxies do not substitute for it.
nodes.evidence: Literature supports learned frozen-backbone connectors, but current LatentWire evidence has not tested tokenwise target loss on ARC.
nodes.next_action: Train frozen-endpoint 16-64 query connector with target loss and strict source-destroying controls.

nodes.claim: LatentWire is faster or more memory-efficient than native KV/cache baselines.
nodes.decision: Block native speed/HBM/goodput claims until measurements exist.
nodes.next_action: Run NVIDIA vLLM/SGLang/C2C/KVComm/KVCOMM/QJL/TurboQuant rows through the validator.

paper_readiness: COLM workshop plausible; ICLR full paper blocked.

subagent_synthesis.decision: Implement a new isolated ARC/OpenBookQA soft-prefix preflight instead of editing core bridge modules.
subagent_synthesis.finding: Repo already has SVAMP target-loss soft-prefix and tokenwise query diagnostics, but ARC/OpenBookQA lack target-loss connector infrastructure.

subagent_synthesis.decision: Use Prefix-Tuning, BLIP-2/Flamingo/Perceiver IO, C2C/KVComm, QJL, and TurboQuant as mandatory boundaries.
subagent_synthesis.finding: Novelty survives only under per-example fixed-byte source-conditioned messaging, frozen/train-only receivers, and source-destroying controls.

technical_contributions: Fixed-byte source-private packet protocol with destructive controls.
Public-basis benchmark gates plus negative/falsification ladder.
Systems byte/exposure accounting against KV/cache transfer baselines.

tokenwise_connector_runbook.goal: Verify target-forward soft-prefix training code on 8-16 ARC rows without claiming evidence.
tokenwise_connector_runbook.name: Mac ARC smoke
tokenwise_connector_runbook.pass_rule: Training runs, controls are emitted, and no source answer/text/KV is transmitted.

tokenwise_connector_runbook.goal: Train/select on ARC validation disagreement rows with frozen source and target endpoints.
tokenwise_connector_runbook.name: ARC soft-prefix preflight
tokenwise_connector_runbook.pass_rule: Matched beats target-only, source-free, zero-source, row-shuffled, same-byte text, and Qwen-substituted controls.

tokenwise_connector_runbook.goal: Train-only receiver/query connector over the strongest OpenBookQA packet-only rows.
tokenwise_connector_runbook.name: OpenBookQA 3B receiver gate
tokenwise_connector_runbook.pass_rule: Packet-plus-receiver beats packet-only by >= +0.005 with positive paired CI95 low and source-destroying controls.

tokenwise_connector_runbook.goal: Evaluate once on frozen larger slices after validation selection.
tokenwise_connector_runbook.name: Frozen ARC/OpenBookQA test gate
tokenwise_connector_runbook.pass_rule: Mean delta >= 0.02 and CI95 low > 0 versus Qwen-substituted, packet-only, target-only, and same-byte text.

tokenwise_connector_runbook.goal: Repeat with one true non-Qwen source-target pair.
tokenwise_connector_runbook.name: Cross-family falsification
tokenwise_connector_runbook.pass_rule: Positive paired CI survives; same-family-only result is marked diagnostic.

tokenwise_connector_runbook.goal: Fill native validator rows for LatentWire and all KV/cache baselines.
tokenwise_connector_runbook.name: Systems ingest
tokenwise_connector_runbook.pass_rule: Validator allows native claims only after complete matched measurements.
